name: Ham and Spinach Double-Stuffed Potatoes
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup frozen chopped spinach, thawed and drained
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 450°F. In a medium nonstick skillet over medium-high heat, heat the oil. Add the ham and onion; cook, stirring, until the onion is soft, about 4 to 5 minutes. Stir in the spinach, salt, and pepper. Remove from heat and set aside.
2. Cut off about half an inch from the long side of each potato. Scoop the cooked potato into a bowl, leaving a 1/4-inch-thick shell. With a fork or potato masher, mash the cooked potato in the bowl. Stir in the ham and spinach mixture, ricotta, and milk until well blended.
3. Spoon the mixture into the shells, dividing evenly and heaping if necessary.
4. Lightly spray a cookie sheet or baking pan with nonstick cooking spray. Place the stuffed potatoes on the sheet. Bake until golden brown, about 15 minutes.
Variation: Halve baked potatoes lengthwise; scoop out the pulp, leaving thin shells. Make the filling as above; spoon the filling back into the potato halves, dividing evenly.
Makes 4 servings. Preparation time: About 25 minutes. Cooking time: About 20 minutes.
